rows.title: Indonesia National AI Roadmap: Government Framework for Artificial Intelligence Development 2020–2045
rows.summary:
The Government of Indonesia has published a National Artificial Intelligence Strategy and Roadmap setting out the country's framework for the development, adoption and governance of artificial intelligence technologies across the period 2020 to 2045, issued under the authority of the National Research and Innovation Agency (Badan Riset dan Inovasi Nasional, BRIN) and the Ministry of State-Owned Enterprises and associated ministries.

The Roadmap was developed with principal coordination from the National Research and Innovation Agency and the Ministry of Communication and Information Technology (Kementerian Komunikasi dan Informatika, Kominfo), with the foundational strategy document released in 2020. The instrument sets out Indonesia's vision for becoming a leading AI-capable nation in South-East Asia by 2045, aligned with the national centenary development vision 'Indonesia Emas 2045'.

The Roadmap identifies five priority sectors for AI deployment: health, bureaucratic reform, education and research, food and agriculture, and mobility and smart cities. It establishes a phased implementation architecture across three periods: an initial capability-building phase (2020–2024), a scaling and integration phase (2025–2039), and a leadership and innovation phase (2040–2045). The document addresses requirements for AI talent development, data infrastructure, computing resources, regulatory frameworks and cross-sector collaboration between government, industry and academic institutions.

On matters of governance, the Roadmap sets out principles for ethical AI development drawing on internationally recognised frameworks, including requirements relating to transparency, accountability, fairness, privacy and security in AI systems. The document identifies the need for a dedicated AI regulatory and standards framework to be developed by competent ministries, and references the existing legal architecture including Law Number 11 of 2008 on Electronic Information and Transactions (UU ITE) and Law Number 27 of 2022 on Personal Data Protection (UU PDP) as foundational instruments governing the digital environment within which AI systems operate.

The Roadmap does not itself constitute binding legislation but operates as a strategic policy instrument guiding ministerial programmes, budget allocations and regulatory development priorities across the relevant planning periods.
